text: When setting project goals, it’s important to use the SMART framework—goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For instance, instead of saying “improve customer service,” set a goal like “reduce response time to under two hours within three months.” This makes success easier to track. You also need to break the project into milestones, which are key checkpoints along the way. Each milestone should lead to a deliverable—a tangible outcome, such as a report or a completed feature. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial: everyone involved must agree on the goals and expectations from the start to avoid confusion later.
